raw_response:
The ideal depth for mineral trioxide aggregate (MTA) as a root-end filling is crucial to ensure adequate sealing without overfilling. Generally, it's recommended not to exceed 3-4mm into the apical region due to concerns about potential microleakage beyond this point.

My answer: B: 3 mm To achieve optimal seal while minimizing risks associated with deeper placement, an ideal depth of MTA for root-end fillings would be around 3 mm. This ensures effective sealing within safe limits. My answer: B: 3 mm. 

This approach balances thorough coverage against reducing potential issues related to overextension beyond the apex.
